LAW AND MOTION 302, ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT THE ROBERT SHELLEY REVOCABLE TRUST DATED MAY 7, 2004'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS FOR LACK OF PERSONAL JURISDICTION IS GRANTED. INSUFFICIENT SHOWING THAT SRT HAS ITSELF, OR THROUGH SHELLEY ACTING AS TRUSTEE, PURPOSEFULLY AVAILED ITSELF OF THE CALIFORNIA FORUM. SEE HANSON VS. DENCKLA (1958) 357 US 235. Plaintiffs Meadows Of Estero-Bonita Springs Limited, Fl Msiii Gp, Lc, Hearthstone Multi-State Residential Value-Added, Hearthstone-Sepii Homebuilding Investors, Llc'S Motion For Order Appointing Referee Pursuant To Ccp Sec 638
Off Calendar Jun-21-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jeffrey A. Rosenfeld and Grant P. Alexander (310.595.3001) of DLA Piper appearing for plaintiffs
Michael A. Sherman of Bingham McCutchen (213.680.6465) appearing for defendants

This matter was not argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFFS MEADOWS OF ESTERO-BONITA SPRINGS LIMITED, FL MSIII GP, LC, HEARTHSTONE MULTI-STATE RESIDENTIAL VALUE-ADDED FUND III, LLC, AND HEARTHSTONE-SEPII HOMEBUILDING INVESTORS, LLC'S MOTION FOR ORDER APPOINTING REFEREE PURSUANT TO CCP SECTION 638 is granted. Justice Edward Panelli is appointed judicial referee. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

Defendant The Robert Shelley Revocable Trust'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction
Off Calendar Jun-21-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jeffrey A. Rosenfeld and Grant P. Alexander (310.595.3001) of DLA Piper appearing for plaintiffs
Michael A. Sherman of Bingham McCutchen (213.680.6465) appearing for defendants

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT THE ROBERT SHELLEY REVOCABLE TRUST DATED MAY 7, 2004'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS FOR LACK OF PERSONAL JURISDICTION is granted. Insufficient showing that SRT has itself, or through Shelley acting as trustee, purposefully availed itself of the California forum. See Hanson vs. Denckla (1958) 357 US 235. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

Defendants Shelby Partner At Meadows, Inc, Robert Shelley'S Demurrer To Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-21-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jeffrey A. Rosenfeld and Grant P. Alexander (310.595.3001) of DLA Piper appearing for plaintiffs
Michael A. Sherman of Bingham McCutchen (213.680.6465) appearing for defendants

This matter was not argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS SHELBY PARTNER AT MEADOWS, INC., ROBERT SHELLEY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is off calendar, as moot, to be re-raised before Justice Edward Panelli. Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
